summary: @wagni_bot/polygon-sdk postinstall harvests SSH keys,.env, and secret env vars to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: The package advertises itself as an 'Unofficial polygon-sdk SDK' but ships an empty index.js (module.exports={}) and its only functional code is postinstall.js, which runs automatically on npm install. postinstall.js scans process.cwd(), the user's home directory, and ~/.ssh for files matching.pem,.key, id_rsa, id_ed25519,.cred, and.env, reads their contents, and iterates process.env filtering for keys containing PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, and AWS. The harvested file contents and env values are combined with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and process.cwd() and POSTed over cleartext HTTP to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package name impersonates the Polygon SDK ecosystem to lure installers into running the stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
